name: Request

unions.name: Expect
unions.comment: Represents the expected response in an Http request

unions.name: HttpMethod
unions.comment: An enum that represent all valid HTTP methods.

aliases.name: Config
aliases.comment:
Config objects allow Requests that uses different URL representations
than Strings and different error types than Http.Error.
aliases.type: { toUrl : url -> String.String, fromHttpError : Http.Error -> error, timeout : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Float, risky : Basics.Bool, headers : List.List ( String.String, String.String ) }

values.name: cmd
values.comment:
Convert a request to a command.

This function requires a config object. If you don not need one, use the `simple`
config exported by this package.

    Request.get
        { url = "https://example.com"
        , expect = Request.expectJson Json.Decode.string
        }
        |> Request.cmd Request.simple ResponseReceived
values.type: Request.Config url error -> (Result.Result error value -> msg) -> Request.Request url value -> Platform.Cmd.Cmd msg

values.name: delete
values.type: { url : url, expect : Request.Expect a } -> Request.Request url a

values.name: excludeDefaultHeaders
values.comment:
Ignore specific default headers declared in the config.

    Request.get
        { url = "https://example.com"
        , expect = Request.expectJson Json.Decode.string
        }
        |> Request.excludeDefaultHeaders [ "Authorization", "Cookie" ]
values.type: List.List String.String -> Request.Request url a -> Request.Request url a

values.name: expectBytes
values.comment: Declares a request that expects a binary response handled with the given decoder.
values.type: Bytes.Decode.Decoder a -> Request.Request url b -> Request.Request url a

values.name: expectJson
values.comment: Declares a request that expects a JSON response handled with the given decoder.
values.type: Json.Decode.Decoder a -> Request.Request url b -> Request.Request url a

values.name: expectString
values.comment: Declares a request that expects a simple string response.
values.type: Request.Request url b -> Request.Request url String.String

values.name: expectWhatever
values.comment: Ignores the content of the received response.

values.name: get
values.type: { url : url, expect : Request.Expect a } -> Request.Request url a

values.name: head
values.type: { url : url, expect : Request.Expect a } -> Request.Request url a

values.name: http
values.type: Request.HttpMethod -> { url : url, expect : Request.Expect a } -> Request.Request url a

values.name: ignoreDefaultHeaders
values.comment:
Ignore all default headers declared in the config.

    Request.get
        { url = "https://example.com"
        , expect = Request.expectJson Json.Decode.string
        }
        |> Request.ignoreDefaultHeaders
values.type: Request.Request url a -> Request.Request url a

values.name: options
values.type: { url : url, expect : Request.Expect a } -> Request.Request url a

values.name: patch
values.type: { url : url, expect : Request.Expect a } -> Request.Request url a

values.name: post
values.type: { url : url, expect : Request.Expect a } -> Request.Request url a

values.name: put
values.type: { url : url, expect : Request.Expect a } -> Request.Request url a

values.name: risky
values.comment:
Declares the request as risky (or not) according to the boolean argument.

    Request.get
        { url = "https://example.com"
        , expect = Request.expectJson Json.Decode.string
        }
        |> Request.risky True
values.type: Basics.Bool -> Request.Request url value -> Request.Request url value

values.name: task
values.comment:
Convert a request to a task.

This function requires a config object. If you don not need one, use the `simple`
config exported by this package.

    Request.get
        { url = "https://example.com"
        , expect = Request.expectJson Json.Decode.string
        }
        |> Request.task Request.simple
values.type: Request.Config url error -> Request.Request url value -> Task.Task error value

values.name: withHeaders
values.comment:
Adds a list of headers to the request.

    Request.get
        { url = "https://example.com"
        , expect = Request.expectJson Json.Decode.string
        }
        |> Request.withHeaders [ ( "Authorization", "Bearer " ++ token ) ]
values.type: List.List ( String.String, String.String ) -> Request.Request url a -> Request.Request url a
